english: the door Dzl., nán-du ytoṅ-ba (nang du gtong ba) to permit to enter. — 2. to let go, i.e. to make go, to send, mi (mi) a man, a messenger, very frq.; _dzaṁ-bui-gliṅ ('dzam bu'i gling) kún-tu (kun tu) btáṅ-nas (btang nas) he made him go all over the country of Dzambuling Dzl.; skyél-du (skyel du) ytoṅ-ba (gtong ba) to dispatch for conveying (a message); lén-du (len du) ytoṅ-ba (gtong ba) to send (a person) for (a thing); _tsól-ba ('tshol ba) btáṅ-ba-las (btang ba las) he sent out searchers (people in search) Dzl. 20, 18., unless this passage should be read _tsól-bar ('tshol bar). — 3. to let have, to give, so in W. almost exclusively; sman (sman) ytoṅ-ba (gtong ba) to give medicine, ytoṅ-tsul (gtong tshul) the way of giving medicine, for 'a dose' Med.; ytoṅ-pod-ćan (gtong phod can) liberal, bounteous Mil.; ytoṅ-sems-ldan (gtong sems ldan) id. S.g.; ytoṅ-sems (gtong sems) liberality, bounty; *toṅ zer* (*tong zer*) he says, give me! he wants to have, he tries to get W.; čós-la (chos la) ytoṅ-ba (gtong ba) to give a person up to religion, i.e. to destine him for the priesthood, to make him take orders. — 4. to make, to cause, e.g. a smoke by lighting a fire Glr.; with the termin. to turn into, byé-taṅ (bye thang) néu-saṅ-du (ne'u gsang du) sandy plains into meadows Glr.; rims(-nad) (rims (-nad)) ytoṅ-ba (gtong ba) to cause, to send down, epidemics, plagues (of gods); to construct, fix, place, chains before a building Glr.; in W. *(s)kad táṅ-če* ((s)kad tang ce) to utter sounds, *kú-ćo (ku co), bó-ra ('bo ra) táṅ-če* (tang ce) to raise, to set up a cry; *kug* (kug) or *kum (kum) táṅ-če* (tang ce) to make crooked, to bend; in forming intensive verbs: *go (mgo) čád (bcad) táṅ-če* (tang ce) to decapitate; *tóṅ toṅ (tong tong), piṅs toṅ (pings tong)* take out! throw out! *tsa toṅ* (tshwa gtong) put salt into it! *ču (chu) táṅ-če* (tang ce) to water (the garden); *lud (lud) táṅ-če* (tang ce) to manure (the fields). The participle *taṅs-pa* (tangs pa) is used adverbially in Ld.; *í-ne (i ne) taṅs-pa (tangs pa) á (a) tsug-pa* (tsug pa) from here to there, from this place to that place (= bzuṅs-te (bzungs te)).

ytód-la (gtod la) mnán-pa (mnan pa), of the sun Mth., of the galaxy Mil., evidently denotes the disappearing of these celestial bodies by enchantment or only as a poetical figure; perh. = ydos (gdos), or to be explained by ytód-pa (gtod pa) II.

I. also ytád-pa (gtad pa), pf. btad (btad), ytad (gtad), fut. ytad (gtad), imp. btod (btod) (Mil.; Cs. tod? (tod?)) 1. to deliver up, lág-tu (lag tu) into the hand, to hand over Glr., to hand to a person the subject for a theme or problem Glr., to commit the management of the household to another Dzl., to commit a child to a teacher Dzl., dge-_dún-la (dge 'dun la) dbaṅ (dbang) to confer important offices on the priesthood Glr., rig-pa (rig pa) to teach; yčig (gcig) snyiṅ (snying) yčig-la (gcig la) ytád-pa (gtad pa) to communicate one's feelings to one another Glr. — 2. to lean against or upon c. dat., e.g. to rest one's head on one's arm; to lay or put against, to, or on, one's mouth to a person's ear Thgr., the tip of the tongue against the palate Gram. — 3. to direct, to turn, mi-la (mi la) mgó-bo (mgo bo), one's face towards a person Lt., mi-la (mi la) mdzúb-mo (mdzub mo), or sdig-mdzúb (sdig mdzub) to point at a person (with the finger) Glr.; sgo (sgo) nub-pyógs (nub phyogs) bál-poi (bal po'i) yúl-du (yul du) ytod (gtod) Glr., the door points south, towards Nepal; _bém-la ('bem la) to take aim, to aim at Lex.; rná-bai (rna ba'i) dbaṅ-po (dbang po) ytód-pa (gtod pa) to listen to, to give a person a hearing Mil.; sems (sems), resp. tugs (thugs), ytód-pa (gtod pa) Mil. id.; _od-zér-la ('od zer la) ytád-nas (gtad nas) yzigs-pas (gzigs pas) turning after a ray of light, following it with the eye (= brtén-nas (brten nas)) Glr.; also used absolutely: dkar-kúṅ (dkar khung) ytód-pa (gtod pa) the projecting windows S.g.(?) — ka (ka) ytód-pa (gtod pa) Glr.? II. inst. of rtód-pa (rtod pa), to fasten (cows etc.) to a stake (driven into the ground), to tedder.
to talk, to speak Sch., cf. ytam (gtam) (?).
filled up, full, for bltáms-pa (bltams pa), ytáms-pa (gtams pa), Sch.

(Lex.) cf. _tór-ba ('thor ba), 1. to strew, to scatter seed, mé-tog-gis (me tog gis) ytór-ro (gtor ro) Dzl. they strewed flowers, also ytór-to (gtor to) Dzl.; ṅá-la (nga la) sas (sas) ytór-ba (gtor ba) they that threw earth upon me Dzl.; sá-la (sa la) ytór-ba (gtor ba) to scatter over the ground Glr. — 2. to cast, to throw, e.g., books into the water Glr., a ring into the air Glr.; to throw out, e.g. spittle into a person's ear, for healing purposes (= _dór-ba ('dor ba)); to cause to circulate the chyle through every part of the body Med.; to waste, to dissipate Dzl., occasionally with the accus. of the vessel containing the substance thrown out: nú- (nu-)
